name: Chano Pozo
abstract: Luciano Pozo González (January 7, 1915 – December 2, 1948), known professionally as Chano Pozo, was a Cuban jazz percussionist, singer, dancer, and composer. Despite only living to the age of 33, he played a major role in the founding of Latin jazz.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: That’s why, when Chano Pozo arrives, that Mario Bauzá introduced him to Dizzy Gillespie... Unfortunately, Chano Pozo gets killed two years later.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: It’s Dizzy and Chano. In 1945 when Chano Pozo came from Cuba, Mario Bauza introduced him to Dizzy Gillespie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Dizzy was the first cat to kind of get into the Latin crossover thing, with Chano Pozo, you know
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I got to hear and learn about Chano Pozo and — you know I’d never met him
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: When Chano Pozo joined the band... we went to the Savoy Ballroom, with Chano Pozo and all that, and we formed -- I think it was the greatest band Diz ever had.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: When I went into the band, he had Chano Pozo... He was in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
